problem.title: Pascal's Triangle II
problem.description: Given an integer rowIndex, return the rowIndex-th (0-indexed) row of Pascal's triangle. Could you come up with an O(rowIndex) space solution?
assumptions: Each value is C(rowIndex, k) for k in 0..rowIndex.

example.explanation: Row 3 of Pascal's triangle. Update a single array in-place from right to left: row[j] += row[j-1]. Going right-to-left avoids using values that have already been updated in this pass.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – In-Place Row Update [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Start with row = [1]. For each level from 1 to rowIndex, update in-place from right to left: row[j] += row[j-1]. Going right-to-left ensures we use the previous row's values before they are overwritten. Append a 1 at the end of each level. Only one O(k) array is ever held in memory.
